channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Killer nightclub hostess's deadly love affair with race car driver was 'eye for an eye': author
item.description: "A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story," a four-part drama series, was inspired by Carol Ann Lee's true-crime book, "A Fine Day for a Hanging: The Real Ruth Ellis Story.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is being buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group is scheduled to be buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Former federal worker shares frustration on TikTok after losing job in less than 2 months
item.description: A federal worker who lost her job after being in her position for less than two months hopped on TikTok to tell her story as well as share information and resources to help other federal workers in the same position.
item.dc:creator: Ya-Marie Sesay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delivery drivers shield children from domestic dispute in Brookfield
item.description: A Brookfield man is in the Trumbull County Jail after allegedly beating and strangling a woman while two children were sheltered in an Amazon delivery truck.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: A federal judge in Manhattan has extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration reverses its previous decision and reinstates legal aid for migrant children
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Days after telling legal groups who help migrant children who arrive in America alone — some so young they are in diapers or their feet dangle from their chairs in court — that they must stop their work, the federal government Friday reversed itself.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON and REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge extends temporary block to huge cuts in National Institutes of Health research funding
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday again blocked the Trump administration’s drastic cuts in medical research funding that many scientists say will endanger patients and delay new lifesaving studies.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AP sues 3 Trump administration officials, citing freedom of speech
item.description: The Associated Press is suing three Trump administration officials over access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: USPS reveals new 'American Vistas' postage stamps
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service revealed two new designs for Presorted Standard stamps in San Diego Friday, illustrating America's iconic natural landscapes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following a press conference with other Proud Boys members and Oath Keepers’ founder Stewart Rhodes.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: Six weeks after devastating wildfires, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the city’s fire chief amid a public rift over preparations for the fires and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chile 'sending their criminals' to U.S., Orange County DA says
item.description: Orange County District Attorney Todd Spitzer says Chilean crime rings are “continuing to terrorize Americans” and is calling for Chile to be removed from a U.S. visa waiver program.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Former Kentucky AG Daniel Cameron launches Senate bid
item.description: Prior to becoming Kentucky’s first Black attorney general, Cameron served as legal counsel to McConnell, the state’s longest serving senator, for two years.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Grand jury recommends Alabama police department be abolished
item.description: The small Alabama city of Hanceville, located about 45 miles north of Birmingham, placed its entire police force on administrative leave a day after the grand jury recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Delta releases new information about captain, first officer flying plane that crashed in Toronto
item.description: Delta Air Lines released new details about the crew aboard flight 4819, operated by its subsidiary Endeavor Air, which crash-landed in Toronto on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of homicide in shooting death of man in crisis
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy was convicted of criminally negligent homicide Thursday in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help after his car got stuck in a small mountain community.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CEO confidence hits highest point in 3 years: Survey
item.description: Forty-four percent in The Conference Board survey said economic conditions have become more preferable “than six months ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Owners of Abby's Bakery in Los Fresnos charged with harboring illegal migrants
item.description: According to the complaint, the apprehension of the individuals was the result of a worksite enforcement action.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Yañez, NewsNation

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: 3 victims killed in Green River Tunnel crash identified, 18 people treated for injuries
item.description: The three people who died in a fiery crash in Wyoming's Green River Tunnel on Friday, Feb. 14 have been identified by officials.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
